title: Self-Supervised Stint Embeddings As Sidecar Features
summary: Recent time-series pretraining work shows that masked/contrastive sequence encoders learn transferable temporal structure, and tabular time-series transformers can pretrain embeddings for downstream tasks.
rationale: Recent time-series pretraining work shows that masked/contrastive sequence encoders learn transferable temporal structure, and tabular time-series transformers can pretrain embeddings for downstream tasks. This dataset is lap-sequential but the current pipeline is almost entirely handcrafted tabular features.
implementation_hint: Build short sequences per `Driver,Race,Stint` over core numeric columns plus compound tokens; pretrain a tiny encoder with masked next-step reconstruction or Siamese past-to-current consistency, then export the current hidden state or pooled embedding as 8-32 numeric features for the existing tree model.
expected_effect: Should capture latent stint shape, warm-up, and degradation trajectory that fixed ratios miss, while keeping the final predictor tabular and simple.
risk: The sequence depth is modest and a neural sidecar can add complexity without beating a strong handcrafted baseline.
